---
title: "Ozan Ozbeker"
subtitle: "Data Scientist & Adjunct Professor"
image: www/images/professional_picture.jpg
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
about:
  template: solana
  image-width: 25em
  links: 
    - icon: github
      text: "GitHub"
      href: https://github.com/ozanozbeker/
    - icon: linkedin
      text: "LinkedIn"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ozanozbeker/
    - icon: envelope
      text: "Email"
      href: mailto:ozanozbeker99@outlook.com
comments: false
---

Hello! I'm Ozan, an industrial engineer turned data scientist. My passion for building and problem-solving started early, from playing with Legos as a kid to crafting worlds in Minecraft. That same drive led me to industrial engineering and, eventually, to the world of data science, where I combine creativity with technology to tackle complex challenges.

I'm currently based in [Morgantown, WV](https://wvtourism.com/morgantown/), working remotely at [OneMagnify](https://onemagnify.com) and teaching as a lecturer at [West Virginia University](https://wvu.edu).

When I’m not immersed in data or coding, you’ll likely find me at a live music event, especially if it’s Metal(core) or EDM. If I’m not at a concert, I’m probably cycling, playing the guitar, or experimenting with a new data science project.

On this site, you’ll find my [resume](resume/index.qmd), [projects](projects/index.qmd), and [blog](blog/index.qmd), where I explore tools and technologies in the data world alongside other topics that spark my curiosity.

Feel free to reach out and connect—I’d love to hear from you!
